articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Agency Capabilities
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with significantly improved agentic task completion. The model demonstrates substantial gains on graduate-level benchmarks and can autonomously execute multi-step workflows across web browsers and code environments. Enterprise API pricing reflects a new tiered compute model based on reasoning depth.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Models Enable Zero-Shot Dexterous Manipulation
articles.summary: DeepMind published results from its Gemini Robotics program showing foundation models that allow robotic hands to perform zero-shot dexterous manipulation of novel objects using vision-language grounding. The system transfers sim-to-real with minimal fine-tuning and achieved 87% success rates on previously unseen household tasks. This represents a major step toward general-purpose robotic manipulation without task-specific training.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude's Real-Time Computer Use API for Enterprise Automation
articles.summary: Anthropic released a production-grade API enabling Claude to interact directly with desktop environments, browsers, and enterprise software in real time with robust guardrails. The system includes a verified action framework that requires human-in-the-loop confirmation for sensitive operations while allowing autonomous execution of routine workflows. Early enterprise partners report 40-60% reductions in repetitive knowledge-work time.

articles.title: Figure and BMW Scale Humanoid Robot Deployment to Full Assembly Line Operations
articles.summary: Figure's second-generation humanoid robots are now operating across multiple BMW manufacturing lines in South Carolina, handling parts logistics, quality inspection, and light assembly tasks alongside human workers. The deployment marks the largest commercial humanoid workforce to date, with over 200 units running three shifts. Figure credits breakthroughs in whole-body control and on-device vision-language models for the reliability improvements needed for production use.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm GAA Process Node Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: TSMC announced it has entered volume production on its A14 (1.4nm-class) gate-all-around transistor process, several months ahead of original projections, with Apple and Nvidia as lead customers. The node delivers approximately 20% performance improvement and 30% power reduction compared to N2. This acceleration tightens the competitive window for Samsung and Intel, both of whom have faced repeated delays on comparable nodes.

articles.title: Meta Releases Llama 4 Behemoth as Open-Weight Model with Mixture-of-Experts at Frontier Scale
articles.summary: Meta released Llama 4 Behemoth, a massive mixture-of-experts open-weight model that matches or exceeds proprietary frontier models on major reasoning and coding benchmarks. The model uses a sparse architecture activating roughly 100B parameters per forward pass from a total of over 1.5T parameters, making it feasible to run on multi-GPU server configurations. The open release has sparked immediate adoption across the research community and is expected to shift competitive dynamics in the foundation model landscape.

articles.title: Neuralink Receives FDA Clearance for Second-Generation Brain-Computer Interface with Bidirectional Stimulation
articles.summary: The FDA has granted Neuralink clearance to begin human trials of its N2 implant, which adds targeted electrical stimulation capabilities to the existing high-bandwidth neural recording platform. The bidirectional interface could enable not only motor output for paralyzed patients but also sensory feedback, a critical step toward practical closed-loop neuroprosthetics. The trial will enroll 50 participants with spinal cord injuries across five U.S. medical centers.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Hyundai Unveil Atlas Electric Platform with Autonomous Construction Capabilities
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics demonstrated its latest Atlas electric humanoid performing autonomous construction tasks including rebar tying, concrete form inspection, and heavy material transport on an active Hyundai E&C building site in Seoul. The robot uses a combination of LiDAR-based SLAM, vision-language planning, and reinforced whole-body locomotion to navigate unstructured job sites. Hyundai plans to deploy pilot fleets to address South Korea's acute construction labor shortage by early 2027.
